cards.notes: Starter strike, replace when possible
Starter defend, replace when possible
Starter summon 5 skill. Built-in barricade via Aussie HP carrying over turns
Starter attack, damage scales with Aussie HP. Blocking keeps Aussie alive = more damage
Summon 6 exhaust for 1 energy. Basically a worse starter effect with exhaust. Never exciting
33 AoE for 6 energy, reduced by 2 per ethereal played. Often 33 to all for 0 since good cards happen to be ethereal. Wins long fights
1 energy deal damage + apply doom equal to damage. ~16 effective damage. Better ways to get this effect; doom doesn't scale like poison
1 energy 9 AoE + 9 block but Aussie dies. Bottom of A. Aussie dying is real downside when stacking HP, but early game Aussie is often dead anyway. Upgrades to 12/12
0 energy gain 1 energy, apply 3 doom to self. Doom on self barely matters. Two-card infinite with Capture Spirit. Upgrade to 2 energy makes any deck infinite
52 damage for 4 energy. Almost never playable — basically a curse. Clunky, doesn't block, payoff isn't worth the draw reliance
Power: Aussie attacks deal 4 additional damage. Aussie attack package is the weakest archetype. Mediocre, rarely needed
1 energy rare power: add 1 random ethereal card to hand each turn. Hello World that doesn't clog. One-card solve for long fights. Random gen is strong when you're blocking all the time
1 energy, enemy loses 3 HP, add 3 souls to draw pile. Two-card infinite with Borrowed Time. 6 draw worth of stuff for 1 energy. Completely overstated
1 energy summon 3 + exhaust 1 card from draw pile. Targeted exhaust is incredibly strong for thinning to infinite. Also gives barricade block
1 energy power: apply 6 doom to random enemy each turn. Essentially 6 passive damage/turn. Opens all doom synergies (Death's Door, etc). Well-designed bridge card. Could move up
1 energy power: gain 3 block when playing 2+ cost card. Can't proc more than once per turn. Worse than Metallicize. So much work for no payoff
2 energy apply 21 doom + 1 weak to all enemies. Top of A, could argue S. Weak is crazy in this game. Big AoE doom number. Great early game pickup
1 energy deal 8 + 3 per card drawn this turn. 8 damage floor is decent consolation. Scales huge with soul chains (24+ damage). Solid early damage card
1 energy gain 6 block, if doom applied this turn gain block 2 additional times (=18 block). Works great with Countdown. Massive block. Bottom of A
1 energy deal 7, vuln+weak are 2x effective for 3 turns. Weak now blocks for half instead of 25%. Disgusting card. Invalidates enemy damage for 3 turns
1 energy ethereal deal 13 (upgrades 17). Hemokinesis without downside. Ethereal is upside on this character. Solid early damage
1 energy ethereal gain 6 block + 1 weak. Best common in the class. Upgraded: 2 weak 7 block = effectively block 20 for 1 energy. Ethereal is pure upside. Take multiple copies. Slam on sight
2 energy gain 11 block, next turn gain 1 energy. OK card but Defy exists at effectively block 20 for 1 energy, making this unnecessary. No ethereal keyword
3 energy ethereal power: gain 1 energy + draw 1 additional card each turn. Upgrades to 2 energy cost. Extra energy and draw every turn is broken. Both resources at once
1 energy power: whenever you play a soul, summon 1 (upgrades to 2). Every soul = barricade block that also scales starter card damage. Top of A, needs soul synergy but payoff is massive
X cost summon 3 X times + add X souls to draw pile. Main reason infinite is so easy. Scales damage, blocks, and adds draw simultaneously. 'Durge slop' with Borrowed Time
1 energy deal 10 + upgrade 2 random cards in discard. Janky scaling solution. Too slow most of the time. Some early game damage value
1 energy put 3 cards from discard into hand, exhaust. Top of B. Sets up combo pieces (Borrowed Time, souls, defy). Exhausts itself. Good when fights go long
2 energy exhaust hand, 9 cards exhausted = intangible. Nine-card threshold rarely met without souls. But exhausting hand enables infinite faster. Exhaust statuses
3 energy apply 29 doom to all + kill enemies with doom >= HP. Bottom of A. Enough to kill early/mid. Kills millipedes in act 2. Character struggles with that otherwise
Ethereal, enemy loses 8 strength this turn. Could argue S tier. Massive block output vs multi-attackers. If you don't need it, it exhausts itself via ethereal
X cost retain, deal 11 damage X times. Top of B. Incredibly buffed Skewer with retain. Hold for big turns. Rare rarity bumps it down
Ethereal deal 7 + apply 1 vuln. Bottom of B. Ethereal keyword turns on Banshee's Cry. Upgrades to 2 vuln. Decent filler
0 energy Aussie deals 3 damage, first time each turn draw 1 card. Free damage + free draw. Like FTL but doesn't turn off. Bridges into Aussie synergy
2 energy Aussie deals 12, costs 0 if Aussie attacked this turn. Desperation damage. 2 energy brick in bad draw order. Aussie attack package is weakest
Eternal power: at end of combat remove a card from deck. Removing cards is completely broken — just go infinite. Build-defining
1 energy power: lose 2 strength, gain 1 energy each turn. Bottom of S. Strength loss doesn't matter since damage comes from Aussie/doom/effects. Basically free energy every turn
Multiplayer card, not rated in tier list
1 energy deal 4 damage, put card from discard to hand, exhaust. Damage hologram. Never felt the need to pick it
1 energy gain 8 block + add soul to draw pile. Common. 8 block + draw 2 (via soul) is ridiculous value. Upgrades to soul+ (draw 3) and 10 block
1 energy deal 10, double damage of all Hang cards. Claw but good. One-card solve for boss damage. 10 baseline is solid. Rare
1 energy power: whenever you play a soul, random enemy loses 6 HP. Infinite payoff but also good without. Capture Spirit = 18 free damage
2 energy deal 11 + apply 2 vuln to all. Bottom of B, maybe C. AoE vuln is OK but 2 energy cost is high
1 energy: next turn summon 2 + gain 2 energy. Top of B, maybe bottom A. Like Outmaneuver but better. Extra energy on barricade character is strong. Upgrade: summon 3, 3 energy
Multiplayer card, not rated in tier list
1 energy ethereal power: first attack each turn deals 50% additional damage. Often only playing one attack (starter). Stacks additively. Combos with Eradicate and Hang
3 energy gain 13 block, cost reduces by 1 whenever anyone dies (including Aussie). Top of B. Can become 0-cost 13 block through Aussie death loops
0 energy deal 7 + apply enemy's debuffs to all others. Transfer doom/weak/strength down to all. Free damage. A or high B, may be slightly overrated
2 energy rare power: summon 5, whenever Aussie loses HP all enemies lose that much HP. Bottom of B. Weird AoE that never really popped off. Doesn't work with Bone Shards kill
1 energy gain 5 block + apply 7 doom to all enemies. Common. Upgrades to 7 block / 11 doom. Block + AoE damage at common rarity is insane
0 energy draw 2 + gain 3 energy next turn, apply 3 doom to self. Essentially Offering/Adrenaline. Doom barely matters, sometimes even positive. Just ridiculous
1 energy apply 10 doom + 5 per 10 doom already on enemy. Uncommon that kills bosses. Bad frontload but scales. Tech card for boss matchups
0 energy whenever you play a card apply 3 doom. C or D. Worse payoff than Haunt. Doom doesn't kill until next turn. Not a power so it clogs
1 energy power (upgrades to 0): whenever you draw ethereal card, draw 1 more. Fills hand constantly. At 0 cost very easy to carry. Consistency engine
1 energy ethereal draw 3. Just Skim. Hard to justify when Grave Warden exists. Good draw rate but wants block stapled on
0 energy Aussie deals 6 damage. Lets you deal damage while spending energy on blocking. Bridges into Aussie synergy. Simple honest card
1 energy Aussie deals 10 + additional damage equal to Aussie max HP. Disgusting card in a vacuum. Ancient rarity so competes against boss relics
2 energy summon 4 + gain 7 block. Bottom of B. Block 11 total with some carrying over. Block commons have better rate
1 energy deal 5 per ethereal card played this combat. Top of C. Slow scaling. Souls don't count (exhaust not ethereal). Better options exist
1 energy apply 2 weak + 2 vuln, exhaust. Upgrades to 3 of each. Debuffs are just good. Weak invalidates enemies
1 energy Aussie deals 7, hits additional time per other Aussie attack this turn. Hard to get payoff. Defile exists at 13 with no condition
3 energy summon 20, exhaust. Big barricade block that carries over turns. Like Impervious with Barricade. Expensive. Honest middle-of-pack card
3 energy retain deal 27. Bottom of B. Retaining big attack for act 1 kills. Only draw it once if you don't need it
3 energy power: attacks also apply doom equal to damage dealt. Top of A, bottom of S. Doubles your damage output. Expensive but worth it in boss fights
1 energy deal damage + add soul to draw pile. Honest damage common like Pommel Strike. Does the thing
0 energy Aussie deals 4 damage, returns to hand when you play 2+ cost card. Strange, hard to get pieces working
1 energy retain: if Aussie alive he dies + gain block equal to double his max HP. Bad early, strong later. Upgrades to 0 energy. Enables big power plays
1 energy apply 13 doom + draw 1 card. Upgrades to 16 doom draw 2. Damage common that draws. Gets through shields. Fine
Deal 8 damage + add ethereal to a card in hand. Low damage number. Ethereal to hand doesn't work well with Page Storm. Weird card
0 energy ethereal: transform a card in draw pile into a soul. Immediately enables infinite. Exhausts itself when deck is empty. 'Don't know why this is in the game'
2 energy rare power: add Sweeping Gaze (0 cost Aussie 10 damage) to hand each turn. Way worse than Countdown. 'Why is this rare?'
2 energy deal 13 + add soul to hand, draw pile, and discard pile. Makes souls in multiple zones. Can enable loops
0 energy: lose 2 strength, enemy loses 2 strength, exhaust. Strength downside doesn't matter. Zero-cost Disarm. Great block output increase
Power: whenever you apply doom gain 2 block (upgrades to 3). Top of B, maybe A. Solid with doom package. Depends on deck composition
1 energy Aussie deals 5 + summon 2 whenever Aussie hits this enemy this turn. D or C. Aussie attack package is weakest. Never had a run where this worked
2 energy power: whenever you apply debuff, enemy takes 9 damage. Expensive but AoE with debuff cards. Doom counts as a debuff
1 energy deal 7 + add retain to a card in hand. Retain is good but 7 damage too low. Want to retain while blocking, not attacking
1 energy deal 9 + 2 per soul in exhaust pile. Top of B. 9 damage baseline is decent. Boss killing card. Rare
1 energy retain deal 8 to all enemies. Cleave with retain. Solid act 1 AoE pickup
Power: whenever you play ethereal gain 4 block. Mental Fortress equivalent. Procs at least once per turn with Defy. With Defy = blocking ~25. Floor is very good
1 energy retain summon 3 + Aussie heals 5 HP. Bottom of A. Retain is great. Upgraded: summon 5 heal 7 = 12 barricade block for 1 energy
Aussie deals 25 + 5 per other Aussie attack this turn. D or C. Aussie attack package is weakest archetype. Bridge cards for this package aren't strong enough
2 energy deal 13, permanently increases damage by 3, exhaust. Ritual Dagger but doesn't need lethal. Meta scaling that snowballs. Need it early
2 energy deal damage equal to enemy's doom, exhaust. Can kill bosses but hard to combo. Situational boss-kill card
1 energy exhaust: add replay to a card in hand (costs extra energy). Top of C. Replay is strong but very situational. Needs miracle draw orders
0 energy gain 7 block + add copy to discard pile. Decent frontload block. Rare. Needs targeted exhaust to control cloning. Awkward in bosses
1 energy deal 10 + next ethereal card costs 0. Solid damage + energy cheating. Can cheat Demesne into play. Decent card
0 energy gain 1 energy, exhaust. Upgrade doesn't go to 2 energy. Low-impact. Too often a brick. Rather have reusable energy like Borrowed Time
